content.introduction: The Federal Reserve kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 3.5–3.75%, but dissent among policymakers reached its highest level since 1992. This divergence in views occurred during the Federal Open Market Committee meeting on April 29, 2026. Three policymakers opposed the meeting's language signaling a bias toward cuts.
body: CNBC (@CNBC). 38 likes 15 replies. Fed holds rates steady but with highest level of dissent since 1992.
The Fed held its key interest rate steady in a range between 3.5%-3.75%. Here are the details: cnb.cx/4ddD6Rs.
The Federal Reserve held rates, but dissent hit its highest level since 1992 as three policymakers opposed language signaling a bias toward cuts
